failingTest: getTagDef does not match uppercase/mixed-case SSML tag names
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts — modify — change exact comparison to lowercase-normalized comparison in getTagDef
apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts — modify — add uppercase and mixed-case test cases for getTagDef and isTagSupportedByProvider
fixApproach: In getTagDef(), lowercase both the input tagName and the candidate t.tag before comparing. This is a one-line change: `tagName.toLowerCase() === t.tag.toLowerCase()`. The existing `isTagSupportedByProvider` function calls `getTagDef` internally and needs no changes. Add test cases in tags.test.ts for uppercase ('PROSODY') and mixed-case ('Break') lookups, plus a negative case ('nonexistent') to confirm undefined is still returned.
steps: Open apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts and locate getTagDef function
Change the comparison from `t.tag === tagName` to `t.tag.toLowerCase() === tagName.toLowerCase()`
Add test cases: getTagDef('PROSODY') returns prosody def, getTagDef('Break') returns break def, getTagDef('emphasis') returns emphasis def, getTagDef('nonexistent') returns undefined
Add test cases: isTagSupportedByProvider with uppercase/mixed-case tag names matches the lowercase equivalent
Run `npm run test` to verify all existing and new tests pass
summary: Make getTagDef() case-insensitive so SSML tag lookups work regardless of casing
